response_text:
# Common HTTP Status Codes

## 1xx — Informational
| Code | Name | Meaning |
|------|------|---------|
| 100 | Continue | Server received request headers, client should proceed |
| 101 | Switching Protocols | Server is switching protocols as requested |

## 2xx — Success
| Code | Name | Meaning |
|------|------|---------|
| **200** | OK | Request succeeded |
| **201** | Created | Resource successfully created |
| 204 | No Content | Success but no body to return |
| 206 | Partial Content | Partial resource returned (used in file downloads) |

## 3xx — Redirection
| Code | Name | Meaning |
|------|------|---------|
| 301 | Moved Permanently | Resource has a new permanent URL |
| **302** | Found | Temporary redirect |
| 304 | Not Modified | Cached version is still valid |
| 307 | Temporary Redirect | Same as 302 but preserves HTTP method |
| 308 | Permanent Redirect | Same as 301 but preserves HTTP method |

## 4xx — Client Errors
| Code | Name | Meaning |
|------|------|---------|
| **400** | Bad Request | Malformed or invalid request |
| **401** | Unauthorized | Authentication required |
| **403** | Forbidden | Authenticated but not permitted |
| **404** | Not Found | Resource doesn't exist |
| 405 | Method Not Allowed | HTTP method not supported |
| 409 | Conflict | Request conflicts with current state |
| 410 | Gone | Resource permanently deleted |
| 422 | Unprocessable Entity | Validation errors |
| 429 | Too Many Requests | Rate limit exceeded |

## 5xx — Server Errors
| Code | Name | Meaning |
|------|------|---------|
| **500** | Internal Server Error | Generic server-side failure |
| 501 | Not Implemented | Server doesn't support the functionality |
| **502** | Bad Gateway | Upstream server returned invalid response |
| **503** | Service Unavailable | Server temporarily
